question: What is the Dungeon Train?
narration: Carriage {carriage}. That is where the dark reached you. Do you know it now? The Dungeon Train does not stop — it only goes on, deeper, forever.

question: What is the Dungeon Train?
narration: {carriage} carriages into the dark. Few ride so far before the line takes them. Fewer still board again.

question: What is the Dungeon Train?
narration: Carriage {carriage}. Barely aboard, and already the dark had you. The line is patient — it can afford to be.

question: Why are you here?
narration:
You felled {mobs} things in the dark, and bled for it with {hearts} hearts.
Of the {met} souls you met, {slain} you ended — and {friends} you called friend.

question: What are you meant to do?
narration: Iron and edge, gathered from the dark and carried this far. None of it was enough — perhaps that was never the point.

question: What are you meant to do?
narration: You read {books} of the line's books and still carried steel. Knowledge and a blade, and the dark took both.

question: Were they worth it?
narration: You made {friends} friends on the line, and still it took you. Perhaps that is the only number that was ever yours to keep.

question: How much of the dark did you answer in kind?
narration: {mobs} things fell to your hand before you fell to theirs. The line does not judge. It only counts.

question: These are all good questions
narration: These are not the marks of a single life. Across all {deaths} — every dark you have boarded, every time the line took you and still you returned — this is the whole of your journey.

question: Is one life so easily spent?
subline: your first fall on the line.
narration: There are no other lives to weigh against this one — not yet. This is the whole of you: everything done since first you boarded. The line will remember it. So should you.

question: These are all good questions
narration: These are not the marks of a single life. {deaths} times the dark has taken you, and {deaths} times you boarded again.

question: Will you board again, or step off into the quiet?
narration: The platform is empty. The whistle sounds far down the line. It runs without end — and so, it seems, do you.
epitaph: the {deaths}th to fall. perhaps not the last.

question: Will you board again — now that you know how it ends?
narration: The platform is empty, the same as it was when first you stepped aboard. The whistle sounds far down the line. You have fallen once. Whether there is a second time is, as ever, yours to decide.
epitaph: the first to fall. the line is patient — it will wait for the rest.

question: Need you even ask?
narration: The platform knows your face by now. The whistle sounds the way it always does. You already know what comes next.
epitaph: the {deaths}th to fall — and not, it seems, the last.

question: What is the Dungeon Train?
narration: Carriage {carriage}. The dark had been waiting there all along — you only arrived to meet it.

question: What is the Dungeon Train?
narration: Carriage {carriage}, and no further. The line keeps no grudge and no mercy; it simply keeps going.

question: What is the Dungeon Train?
narration: The lanterns of carriage {carriage} still swing where you fell. The train does not slow to look.

question: What is the Dungeon Train?
narration: You reached carriage {carriage} before the floor of the world gave way. The line carries on without your weight.

question: What is the Dungeon Train?
narration: Carriage {carriage}. Somewhere ahead the track keeps unrolling into the black, indifferent that you are no longer on it.

question: What is the Dungeon Train?
narration: Here, at carriage {carriage}, the dark finally matched your pace. It was always going to.

narration: {mobs} things fell to you and {hearts} hearts fell from you. You crossed {met} other souls in the dark — ended {slain}, kept {friends} — and then it was your turn.

question: Why are you here?
narration: Of the dark you took {mobs}; of yourself you spent {hearts} hearts. {met} souls shared the line: {slain} you ended, {friends} you held onto.

question: Why are you here?
narration: You felled {mobs} things and bled {hearts} hearts. You met {met} souls, ended {slain}, and called not one of them friend. The dark is simpler alone.

question: What did you answer the dark with?
narration: Of the {met} you met, {slain} you cut down — {mobs} things more in the dark besides — for {hearts} hearts, and {friends} you spared to call friend. The line does not call it murder.

question: What are you meant to do?
narration: You gathered all you could hold from the carriages. The line lets you keep it — it has no use for cargo, only for passengers.

question: What are you meant to do?
narration: You cracked not a single crate on the line — carried only what you boarded with, and swung it till the end. The dark keeps what you would not take.

question: What are you meant to do?
narration: A handful of crates opened — {loot} in all — and a blade kept close. The line is generous to those who stop; you barely paused.

question: What are you meant to do?
narration: {loot} containers emptied into your pack. You stripped the dark of all that wasn't nailed down, and still it took the one thing that mattered.

question: What are you meant to do?
narration: Not a page turned — only steel gathered and swung. The line keeps its stories for those who stop to read.

question: What are you meant to do?
narration: You read {books} of the line's books and carried steel besides. Wisdom and a blade, and the dark was patient with both.

question: These are all good questions

question: These are all good questions
narration: Across {deaths} lives the numbers gather like dust on the rails. None of it is lost; the line forgets nothing.

question: Why do you keep boarding?
narration: {deaths} falls, and still you board. The line stopped wondering whether you would return. So, perhaps, have you.

question: Why do you keep boarding?
narration: {deaths} deaths behind you and the same whistle ahead. You are not a passenger anymore — you are part of the timetable.

question: Why do you keep boarding?
narration: The dark has stopped being a stranger; {deaths} times over you have learned its face. That is its own kind of loss.

question: Will you board again, or let the platform keep you?
narration: The whistle is already sounding, far ahead. It is not waiting for you — it never was — and yet here you stand, deciding to follow.
epitaph: the {deaths}th to fall. the line goes on.

question: Will you board again?
narration: The platform is quiet. Somewhere the train is already full of someone else's story. There is always room for one more of yours.
